items.text: schemas/scout_report.json, dev_report.json, and review_report.json exist in the package and are byte-identical to their docs/design-system/schemas/ canon files.
scripts/check-schema-drift.sh fails loudly when any package stage-contract schema diverges from canon, and runs as part of the test gate.
src/aion_stacked_dev_io.gleam is generated by aion codegen from the package schemas, carries the do-not-edit header, and aion codegen --check passes clean in CI.

Brief document types (authored fields + optional scout/dev/review per requirement + optional execution block) are defined in Gleam with hand-written codecs that round-trip the cluster's own brief files (fixture: docs/design/brief-dev/briefs/BD-001.json) losslessly with deterministic encoding.
Resolved-context types (ADR texts for design_anchor, C#/S# texts, roadmap provenance quote) are defined with codecs; workflow code never sees an unresolved reference.

prompts.gleam exposes pure functions scout_prompt, dev_prompt, review_prompt, and resume_feedback over the brief document, resolved context, and explicit stage inputs (prior stage reports, the attestation, measured diagnostics) — no filesystem access, no ambient state.
Each projection renders anchored ADRs as 'id: title — decision' lines and verbatim quotes as quoted words with speaker attribution, never paraphrased.
Unit tests pin each projection's output for a fixture brief and assert character budgets: scout ≤ 6000, dev ≤ 9000, review ≤ 12000.

The dev projection renders scout blocks inline per requirement; the review projection renders the dev blocks, the dev attestation, and the measured check results — never the scout (ADR-010: the reviewer verifies the dev diff against the brief with fresh eyes).
src/brief_dev.gleam exists as the inner-child entry module and src/onatopp_dev.gleam is deleted along with its workflow.toml entry and schemas (no parallel family).
The scout stage runs the scout activity (read-only norn, session '<branch>-scout', scout-report output schema) before any dev work.
The dev stage preserves the concurrent warm_build + dev fan-out via the tagged StartupTask envelope and the dev session id remains '<branch>'.
The verify-fix loop preserves onatopp_dev semantics: scoped_checks then dev_resume with diagnostics, bounded by required verify_fix_cap with required round_backoff_ms sleeps.
The review stage runs the dev_review activity (norn reviewer, session '<branch>-review', review-report output schema) after verify convergence, never sharing the dev session.
When the review report's fixes are non-empty, the pipeline re-runs scoped_checks; a harden pass that breaks verification fails the run with a typed error naming the review stage.
Any requirement left with alignment 'drifted' in the review report fails the run with a typed error listing the drifted R# ids and their issues.
brief_dev re-registers its status query at every stage transition with phases: scouting, developing, verifying, fixing, reviewing, hardening, converged.

BriefDevResult carries the scout, dev, and review reports plus verify_rounds and build_warm; BriefDevError covers scout failure, dev block, verify exhaustion, review drift, harden regression, and stage failure.
enrich.gleam merges a stage report into the brief document purely and append-only: replacing that stage's blocks wholesale, never merging field-by-field, never touching authored fields.
The enrich_brief activity deep-compares the authored subset of the on-disk worktree brief against the document it was handed and fails terminally on any divergence before writing.

Enrichment is written into the worktree brief file after scout, after dev convergence, and after review; the execution block (gate, attestation, review_verdict, branch, session, status, landed fields) is written before land.
Enrichment merge round-trips through validate.py: an enriched fixture brief validates against brief.schema.json.

StackedDevInput carries the v2 brief document and resolved context in place of the four document strings; the input schema mirrors it with additionalProperties false and every cap/deadline still required.
stacked_dev spawns brief_dev (not onatopp_dev) and lifts its typed errors into the outer union; the gate child, review loop, signal contract, and land sequence are unchanged.
The landed merge contains the enriched brief file: implementation and execution record arrive on main in the same commit lineage.

src/dispatch.gleam exists as a third entry module with workflow.toml entry and input/output schemas; its input names the wave (brief ids) plus required design_dir, repo_root, base_ref, reviewers, caps, and deadlines — the assemble_wave activity produces the ordered, fully resolved wave before any workflow logic sees it.

A dispatcher activity assembles the wave outside workflow code: reads roadmap.json and the cluster documents, resolves all references, orders by depends_on, and refuses briefs whose depends_on have not landed or whose coverage check fails.

dispatch runs stacked_dev children serially in input order, records a per-brief outcome (landed with branch and commit, or failed with the typed error), and continues or halts on failure according to a required halt_on_failure input.
dispatch re-registers a status query per child transition answering current brief id, position, and per-brief outcomes so far.
Worker handlers for scout, dev_review, and enrich_brief mirror locals invocation-for-invocation; SERVED_ACTIVITIES lists every activity name the family dispatches.

Norn scout and review invocations use --print, --session-id or --resume, --output-schema with the generated stage schemas, and --output-format json; output parsing accepts the bare shape and the real {"output": ...} envelope only.
wire_compat.rs pins every payload that exists at its landing byte-compatible both directions against the Gleam codecs; the dispatch IO pins complete with the dispatch brief.

The hermetic suite covers: scout failure, dev block, verify exhaustion, review drift unfixed (run fails), review fixes with passing re-verify, harden regression caught by re-verify, and the full happy path with an enriched brief asserted on the landed branch.

CLI shims emit captured real norn envelopes for scout and review stages; shim argv logs prove session id discipline ('<branch>-scout' / '<branch>' / '<branch>-review') and that diagnostics and review notes travel intact.
A hermetic dispatch test runs a two-brief wave with a depends_on edge and asserts ordering, per-brief outcomes, and halt_on_failure both ways.

The live e2e extends to the dispatch arc: deploy all archives, dispatch a one-brief wave, SIGKILL and restart the server mid-review, drive the review signal after recovery, and assert the enriched brief exists in the merged result with zero re-executed activities.
The dev-pipeline template mirrors every family change with {{name}} placeholders and its rendered-worker gates pass (fmt, clippy -D warnings, tests).

cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, the full workspace suite, the package gleam test suite, and check-schema-drift.sh all pass clean at the cluster's landing commit.
